module.title: MUTATIONS II
module.subtitle: DELETE · UPDATE expr · INSERT…SELECT · conditional
module.theory:
## Rewriting the grid
In the last sector you added rows (INSERT) and changed them (UPDATE). Now you learn to **delete** rows, **compute** new values, **copy** rows between tables, and make **conditional** edits. Every move here is a *mutation* — it changes the database instead of just reading it.

For each task you write the mutation; the verifier then runs a **check** query (a plain SELECT, shown in the prompt) and compares what it reveals. So you are always told exactly what will be inspected afterward.

## DELETE — remove rows
**WHAT:** **DELETE FROM table WHERE condition;** erases every row that matches the condition.
**WHY:** purge dead, expired, or unwanted records.
~~~sql
DELETE FROM drones WHERE status = 'scrapped';
~~~

> WARNING — A DELETE with **no WHERE** wipes the *entire* table. There is no undo here. Always aim with WHERE before you fire.

## UPDATE with an expression
SET can store a **computed value**, not just a fixed one. The right side can do arithmetic on the column's current value.
**WHAT:** **SET battery = battery + 10** reads each row's current battery and writes back the new number.
**WHY:** recharge, apply a discount, add a bonus — change values *relative* to what they already are.
~~~sql
UPDATE drones SET battery = battery + 10 WHERE status = 'active';
~~~

> INTEL — **battery = battery + 10** is not a contradiction. SQL reads the old value, adds 10, and stores the result. It is the same idea as x = x + 10 in other languages.

## INSERT … SELECT — copy rows in
Instead of typing VALUES, you can feed INSERT the **rows a SELECT returns**. The selected columns line up, in order, with the target columns.
**WHAT:** copy matching rows from one table into another.
**WHY:** archive, snapshot, or move records without retyping them.
~~~sql
INSERT INTO scrapyard (codename, status)
SELECT codename, status FROM drones WHERE battery = 0;
~~~

> WARNING — The SELECT's columns must match the INSERT column list in **count and order**, or you store data in the wrong place.

## Conditional UPDATE with CASE
**CASE** lets **one** UPDATE write **different** values per row, based on a test.
**WHAT:** **CASE WHEN test THEN value ... ELSE value END** picks a value row by row.
**WHY:** retag, re-grade, or bucket many rows in a single statement.
~~~sql
UPDATE drones
SET status = CASE WHEN battery = 0 THEN 'dead' ELSE 'active' END;
~~~

> INTEL — Without an **ELSE**, rows that match no **WHEN** are set to NULL. Add ELSE to leave them on a sensible default.

exercises.title: PURGE THE SCRAP
exercises.prompt:
The **drones(id, codename, battery, status, credits)** fleet is cluttered with junk. DELETE from **drones** every row whose **status** is **'scrapped'** — and nothing else.

The verifier then runs: SELECT codename FROM drones ORDER BY codename.
exercises.check: SELECT codename FROM drones ORDER BY codename;
exercises.lore: Two husks, hauled to the smelter before dawn.

exercises.title: DROP THE BROKE
exercises.prompt:
Cut the freeloaders. DELETE from **drones** every row whose **credits** equals **0** — leave every other row untouched.

The verifier then runs: SELECT codename FROM drones ORDER BY codename.
exercises.check: SELECT codename FROM drones ORDER BY codename;
exercises.hint: Add WHERE credits = 0 so you only remove the broke unit.
exercises.lore: No credits, no clearance. Off the grid you go.

exercises.title: RECHARGE THE ACTIVE
exercises.prompt:
A power surge tops up the working fleet. UPDATE **drones**: for every row whose **status** is **'active'**, set **battery** to its current value **plus 10**. Other rows stay as they are.

The verifier then runs: SELECT codename, battery FROM drones WHERE status = 'active' ORDER BY codename.
exercises.starter: UPDATE drones SET battery = 10 WHERE status = 'active';
exercises.solution: UPDATE drones SET battery = battery + 10 WHERE status = 'active';
exercises.check: SELECT codename, battery FROM drones WHERE status = 'active' ORDER BY codename;
exercises.hint: Use SET battery = battery + 10 (read the old value, add 10), not battery = 10.
exercises.lore: Coils hum. Ten more percent before the next sweep.

exercises.title: DOUBLE THE BOUNTY
exercises.prompt:
Management offers a retention bonus. UPDATE **drones**: for every row whose **status** is **'idle'**, set **credits** to **double** its current value (credits times 2). Leave all other rows alone.

The verifier then runs: SELECT codename, credits FROM drones WHERE status = 'idle' ORDER BY codename.
exercises.starter: UPDATE drones SET credits = credits * 2 WHERE status = 'active';
exercises.solution: UPDATE drones SET credits = credits * 2 WHERE status = 'idle';
exercises.check: SELECT codename, credits FROM drones WHERE status = 'idle' ORDER BY codename;
exercises.hint: SET credits = credits * 2, and add WHERE status = 'idle' so only idle units double.
exercises.lore: Idle hands, fattened wallets. Keep them loyal.

exercises.title: ARCHIVE THE DEAD
exercises.brief: Copy flat-battery units to the scrapyard.
exercises.prompt:
Snapshot the casualties. INSERT INTO **scrapyard(codename, status)** the **codename** and **status** of every drone whose **battery** equals **0**. Read the rows with a SELECT from **drones**.

The verifier then runs: SELECT codename, status FROM scrapyard ORDER BY codename.
exercises.starter: -- copy the flat-battery drones into scrapyard with INSERT ... SELECT
exercises.solution:
INSERT INTO scrapyard (codename, status)
SELECT codename, status FROM drones WHERE battery = 0;
exercises.check: SELECT codename, status FROM scrapyard ORDER BY codename;
exercises.hint: INSERT INTO scrapyard (codename, status) SELECT codename, status FROM drones WHERE battery = 0;
exercises.lore: Their last positions, logged in the boneyard ledger.

exercises.title: MOTHBALL THE IDLE
exercises.brief: Archive idle units and drain their battery.
exercises.prompt:
Two-step decommission. First INSERT INTO **scrapyard(codename, status)** the **codename** and the literal status text **'mothballed'** for every drone whose **status** is **'idle'**. Then UPDATE those same **idle** drones in **drones**, setting their **battery** to **0**.

The verifier then runs: SELECT s.codename, s.status, d.battery FROM scrapyard s JOIN drones d ON d.codename = s.codename ORDER BY s.codename.
exercises.starter:
INSERT INTO scrapyard (codename, status)
SELECT codename, status FROM drones WHERE status = 'idle';
exercises.check: SELECT s.codename, s.status, d.battery FROM scrapyard s JOIN drones d ON d.codename = s.codename ORDER BY s.codename;
exercises.hint: Select codename and the literal 'mothballed', then UPDATE ... SET battery = 0 WHERE status = 'idle'.
exercises.lore: Powered down, tagged, and shelved for the long winter.

exercises.title: RETAG BY CHARGE
exercises.brief: One CASE update sets status from battery.
exercises.prompt:
Re-grade the whole fleet in one statement. UPDATE **drones** and set **status** using CASE based on **battery**: battery **0** → **'dead'**; battery **less than 50** (but not 0) → **'low'**; otherwise → **'ready'**. Apply it to every row.

The verifier then runs: SELECT codename, status FROM drones ORDER BY codename.
exercises.solution: UPDATE drones SET status = CASE WHEN battery = 0 THEN 'dead' WHEN battery < 50 THEN 'low' ELSE 'ready' END;
exercises.check: SELECT codename, status FROM drones ORDER BY codename;
exercises.hint: UPDATE drones SET status = CASE WHEN battery = 0 THEN 'dead' WHEN battery < 50 THEN 'low' ELSE 'ready' END; — order the WHENs so 0 is caught first.
exercises.lore: Green, amber, black. The grid recolors itself.

exercises.title: TIER BY CREDITS
exercises.brief: CASE update for high earners only.
exercises.prompt:
Reward the earners — and only them. UPDATE **drones**: for every row whose **credits** is **at least 900**, set **status** using CASE — credits **at least 1000** → **'elite'**, otherwise → **'veteran'**. Rows with credits under 900 must be left exactly as they are.

The verifier then runs: SELECT codename, status FROM drones ORDER BY codename.
exercises.starter: UPDATE drones SET status = CASE WHEN credits >= 1000 THEN 'elite' ELSE 'veteran' END;
exercises.solution: UPDATE drones SET status = CASE WHEN credits >= 1000 THEN 'elite' ELSE 'veteran' END WHERE credits >= 900;
exercises.check: SELECT codename, status FROM drones ORDER BY codename;
exercises.hint: Add WHERE credits >= 900 so the CASE only touches earners; the CASE then splits elite from veteran.
exercises.lore: Two new ranks stamped on the high rollers. The rest stay nameless.
